title: Yan Liberman Discusses Crypto Value Accrual and Hyperliquid's Approach
summary: Yan Liberman argues that judging crypto projects like Venice by traditional value accrual metrics is premature. He highlights Hyperliquid as an example of a project with a strong business model.
body:
In a recent tweet, Yan Liberman responded to critiques about Venice, suggesting that evaluating crypto projects based on immediate value accrual is misguided. He noted that many crypto holders have been burned by tokens lacking real business fundamentals, leading to a demand for instant value accrual.

Liberman mentioned Hyperliquid as a counterexample, implying that the project demonstrates a more sustainable approach to value creation. He did not provide specific details about Hyperliquid's model but emphasized the importance of looking beyond short-term metrics.

This discussion highlights a broader issue in the crypto space: the tension between immediate returns and long-term sustainability. For everyday users, it underscores the need to evaluate projects based on their underlying business models and long-term potential rather than just short-term gains.

As the crypto market continues to evolve, it will be crucial to watch how projects like Venice and Hyperliquid develop their business models and deliver value to their users. Keep an eye on their progress and any announcements they make regarding their long-term strategies.
twitterThread: Crypto's obsession with immediate value accrual might be misguided. Yan Liberman argues projects like Venice need time to develop real business models.
Hyperliquid is an example of a project focusing on sustainable value. Short-term gains shouldn't be the only metric for success in crypto.

For users, it's crucial to look beyond quick profits. Watch how projects evolve their business models. Read more → https://cryptocatalyst.news/articles/2026-06-07-yan-liberman-discusses-crypto-value-accrual-and-hyperliquids-approach
standaloneTweet: Yan Liberman says judging crypto projects like Venice by immediate value accrual is premature. Hyperliquid shows a better way.
